ab: Haiza qudasnau’az han lumaq?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: Uu haiza, han cia sapalan masabaq.
en: Yes, she’s sleeping in her room.

ab: Haiza qudasnau’az han lumaq?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: Uka’, laupang a cia mudata’, nasanavanin cia mucuqais.
ch: B:不在，她剛出去了，要晚上才回家。
en: No, she’s not. She just went out and will be back in the evening.

ab: Haiza qudasnanaz han lumaq?
en: Is grandfather at home?

ab: Han cia dusa’lautu, mundaza’a asu kilim cia ti.
ch: B:他在二樓，你上去找他吧。
en: He’s upstairs. You can go up and find him.

ab: Maiza bunun han lumaq?
en: Is anyone at home?

ab: Uu Bazai qana, talbuntuking nitu pasaduu, macial asu?
ch: B:是Bazai阿，很久沒有看到你了，你好嗎?
en: It’s Bazai. I haven’t seen you in a while! How have you been?

ab: Macial na! maipati caak kilim su’un pababazbaz.
en: I’m doing great! I came to chat with you.

ab: Icaqa tama suu?
en: Where is your father?

ab: Iha’an cia pitdai’an makal’ing i sanglav, luklacan zaku.
ch: B:他在廚房炒菜，我去叫他。
en: He’s in the kitchen cooking. Let me go call him.

ab: munica kat Ival a?
en: Where did Ival go?

ab: Maipa cia tulkukan madamu kis tulkuk.
en: She went to the chicken coop to catch chickens.

ab: Qudasnanaz, maizing caam.
en: Grandfather, we’re here.

ab: Uka’ qudasnanaz muu han lumaq, iha’an cia quma, hanamu lumaq mantatala cia mucuqais.
ch: B:你們的爺爺不在家，他在田裡，你們在家等他回來吧。
en: Your grandfather is not at home. He’s in the field. Wait for him to come back.

ab: Pantamaun maizing asu ki qa!
en: Uncle, you’re here!

ab: Talmuntuking nitu pasaduu, taldaqvasing asu ki.
en: I haven’t seen you in a while. You’ve grown taller.

ab: Mantan manahip pasaduu su’un.
en: It’s so good to see you.

ab: paimuu ca unglai dii, pukumbu’is piskazhavan.
ch: B:這是給你們的鳳梨，要放到冰箱裡。
en: Here’s a pineapple for you. Put it in the refrigerator.

ab: talmuntuking nitu’ pasaduu, luklacunku nai muskun maun qaising ti.
ch: A:這麼久沒來，我叫大家一起來吃飯。
en: It’s been a while. Let’s have a meal together.

ab: Bazing nii, maqtu’ing ca qu’in savil, maipa ata pulungqu’an pababazbaz ti.
ch: B:不用麻煩了，我喝茶就好，我們來客廳聊天。
en: No need to bother. I’ll just have tea. Let’s sit in the living room and chat.

ab: Miqumicang isu tu malkacia tantunguan, namudanin caak.
ch: A:謝謝你的招待，我要回去了。
en: Thank you for your hospitality. I have to go now.

ab: Laupang ca tulkuk dii sintunu, dasi nadaukdaukun suu ma’un.
ch: B:這隻雞剛殺好，你帶回去慢慢吃。
en: This chicken was just freshly killed. Take it home and enjoy it slowly.
